content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

September 11, 2006

Frank Shaw Bacik
Law Offices of Carter,
Oglesby, Momsen & Bacik,
P.O. Box 720
Ukiah, California 95482-0720

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-06-167

Dear Mr. Bacik:

This letter confirms the receipt of your August 9, 2006, request for advice regarding the recusal of Supervisor Michael Delbar from decisions, by the Mendocino County Board of Supervisors, related to the county’s right-of-way across property owned by your clients Susan Schindler and Beatrice Foster.

While the Commission may provide informal assistance, (see regulation 18329(c)(1)), unless you are an authorized representative of the person whose duties under the act are in question, the Fair Political Practices Commission cannot address your questions or provide analysis. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(B) and (c)(1).) Therefore, your request is withdrawn. I enclose a copy of regulation 18329 for your review.

Moreover, if you believe a violation of the Political Reform Act has occurred, you may file a complaint with our Enforcement Division. I have enclosed a copy of “How to Report a Violation of the Political Reform Act” for your review, which details the complaint filing procedure.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By:    Brian G. Lau
        Counsel, Legal Division

Enclosures
BGL:rd
I:\AdviceLtrs\06-167
